name: Cannoli
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons finely minced candied orange peel or citron (optional)
1/4 cup chopped pistachios or other nuts (optional)
recipeInstructions:
Dough:
1. In a mixing bowl using an electric mixer, combine 1 cup flour, sugar, salt, and cinnamon.
2. Attach the dough hook to the mixer and blend on medium-slow speed for about 45 seconds.
3. Add vinegar, water (if needed), egg, and butter. Mix until well combined for 2-3 minutes.
4. Gradually add remaining flour, a quarter cup at a time, until a smooth and elastic dough forms.
5. Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes. If it sticks to the bowl, sprinkle with additional flour. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.
6. Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to 375°F (190°C).
7. Roll out the dough on a floured surface into extremely thin circles (about 1/16 inch or less). Aim for 12-14 circles total.
8. Before placing dough in cannoli tubes, roll each circle slightly to give an oval shape, approximately 5 inches by 4 1/2 inches.
9. Place the longest dimension of the dough along the length of the metal tube and brush with egg white at the tip to seal. Press down gently.
10. Deep fry for about 2 minutes (or until golden brown). Remove with tongs and allow to cool slightly before tapping out the center.
Filling:
1. In a bowl, mix ricotta cheese with a spatula or wooden spoon until smooth. Add powdered sugar, vanilla extract, candied orange peel, chocolate, cinnamon, and orange curacao. Beat until well combined for an additional 4-5 minutes.
2. Chill the filling in the refrigerator until ready to use.
3. Fill each shell using a small spoon. Optionally dip the ends in chopped nuts.
4. Sprinkle with powdered sugar before serving.
Storage:
Unfilled shells can be stored in a cool, dry place for up to four weeks or frozen for three months.
